title: Drone Flight From Bon Accord Lagoon to Nylon Pool Tobago 🇹🇹Follow for more videos #tobago
creator: Fishaholic TT (@FishaholicTT)
notes: TROPICAL ISLAND / TURQUOISE-LAGOON EXEMPLAR (subgenre: tropical island and turquoise-lagoon landscapes) -- first exemplar in this pack for Trinidad & Tobago and for the Bon Accord Lagoon / Nylon Pool area specifically, added following the same per-record verification rigor as this pack's original entries (see e.g. bbc-earth-aerial.json, lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json). A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/ffMC6Fg2pok); per this task's step 1 requirement it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was independently re-verified live in this session via `curl "https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/ffMC6Fg2pok&format=json"`, which returned title="Drone Flight From Bon Accord Lagoon to Nylon Pool Tobago \U0001F1F9\U0001F1F9Follow for more videos #tobago", author_name="Fishaholic TT", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@FishaholicTT", provider_name="YouTube", and vertical embed dimensions height=200/width=113 -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's claimed title, creator, and vertical-Shorts framing, so this session's own primary-source check corroborates rather than merely repeats the prior agent's claim. No Creative Commons, CC0, or other explicit license marking is present anywhere in the oEmbed response (oEmbed does not surface CC licensing metadata at all, so this is not evidence either way, but no corroborating CC claim was found elsewhere in this session or the prior agent's research), so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `license_category` is set to 'all-rights-reserved'. Consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `platform` is 'youtube'. `duration` is left null in this top-level field despite a real, session-verified duration reading in `editorial_style.pacing_notes` (60.021s, read directly from the page's HTMLMediaElement in a prior agent's live-playback pass) -- consistent with this pack's convention (e.g. abc-news-volcano-crater-lava-fpv.json) of reserving the schema's top-level `duration` field for a real YouTube Data API call per reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process, rather than a one-off in-session playback read, and because this session did not itself re-confirm that reading (see editorial_style below for why). `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is a small independent creator's hashtag-driven Shorts upload ('Fishaholic TT', channel @FishaholicTT) with no juried contest, broadcaster, or festival attribution found anywhere in this session's or the prior agent's research -- inventing one here would be fabrication. Subject-distinctiveness note (carried forward from the prior agent's verification_notes): this exemplar is included specifically because it is a genuinely distinct creator, ocean basin, and island nation (Trinidad & Tobago's Bon Accord Lagoon / Nylon Pool, in the Atlantic/Caribbean) from this pack's other tropical-water footage, rather than a redundant repeat of the same visual motif -- this session did not itself re-verify that comparative distinctiveness claim against every other record in the pack (out of scope for a single-record task), so it is attributed to the prior agent's research rather than asserted as independently re-confirmed here. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded or persisted to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint; only a prior agent's transient in-page canvas-frame capture (via Playwright, never saved to disk) touched actual frame pixels. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's existing ~0.70-0.85 range: sharpness=0.75 (a small independent creator with no confirmed drone model or resolution claim anywhere in the verified metadata, placing it below this pack's confirmed-4K/contest-verified tier (e.g. dronestagram-feature.json's 0.85, skypixel11_africa_unseen's 0.80) and closer to its unvetted-personal-account tier (lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json's 0.76, instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json's 0.74) -- nudged slightly above that floor because a prior agent's live-playback pass positively confirmed five separate non-blank, substantial-payload (30-60KB) canvas frame captures spread across the full runtime (at roughly 5%/25%/50%/75%/95%), a genuine corroborating signal of continuous, real, non-degraded aerial footage rather than a title-only claim); exposure=0.75 (a clear-sky, calm, shallow turquoise-lagoon daylight scene -- of the kind exemplified by this pack's other tropical/daylight-water footage, e.g. bbc-earth-aerial.json's tropical-islands compilation at 0.75 -- is a comparatively even, favorable lighting condition versus this pack's harder backlit/sunset or night cases (lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json's 0.70, skywave-city-skyline-blue-hour-night.json's 0.71), so scored at this pack's daylight-favorable tier rather than its harder-lighting tier); motion_smoothness=0.78 (the same five-point live-playback frame capture confirms substantial real footage content persists continuously across the full 60.021s runtime rather than a static title card, freeze-frame, or dropped/black segment at any sampled point -- a genuine, if coarse, positive signal for a single continuous flight/pass rather than an erratic multi-cut edit -- but scored just below this pack's explicitly-stabilized or contest-curated top tier (bbc-earth-aerial.json's 0.80, drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json's 0.80) since no explicit gimbal/stabilization-software claim, e.g. ReelSteady/Gyroflow, was found for this specific clip in this session or the prior agent's research). `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced in this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.75, exposure=0.75, motion_smoothness=0.78, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` via `.venv/bin/activate` then `python3 -c "..."` in this session; `default_weights()` with no explicit version argument resolved to `weights_version='default-v2'` (0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition). Because composition's score is None, `compute_composite_score`'s defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, reducing the result to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.75+0.75+0.78)/3 = 0.76 exactly, confirmed by actually running the function this session rather than hand-computed. `editorial_style` carries forward a prior agent's genuine live-playback review pass (via Playwright MCP tools, since claude-in-chrome was not connected in that session): it navigated to the source_url, confirmed via `document.title` on each load that the correct video ('Drone Flight From Bon Accord Lagoon to Nylon Pool Tobago...', creator Fishaholic TT) was loaded, then read the real HTMLMediaElement.duration = 60.021 seconds (i.e. a full-length 60s YouTube Short, not a brief ~15s clip) and captured canvas frames at t=3.0s/15.0s/30.0s/45.0s/57.0s, all five producing substantial non-blank JPEG payloads (30-60KB at 160x284px) -- confirming continuous real aerial footage plays throughout rather than a static title card or black frames at those sample points. That session was NOT able to persist or visually inspect the captured frame images, nor complete a second finer-grained scrub for cut-boundary detection: on every subsequent re-navigation to the identical URL, the page auto-redirected within seconds to unrelated random YouTube Shorts (a Big Bear Mountain first-snow short, a Maldives overwater-suite short, a city-skyline-at-night short, two different Utah sand-dune shorts) before inspection could finish -- matching this pack's other records' documented anti-bot/feed-redirect failure mode on Shorts URLs (e.g. pshepfpv-iceland-glaciers-fpv.json) -- and a further retry then hit a 'Browser is already in use' automation-lock error, at which point that session stopped per the anti-rabbit-holing review budget rather than retrying further. This session did not itself re-attempt the live-playback pass (no browser-automation tool was invoked here), so the duration/frame-capture findings above are reported as the prior agent's direct observation, not independently re-confirmed pixel-for-pixel in this session -- though the title/creator match via oEmbed described above IS this session's own independent corroboration that the correct video was the one reviewed. Net result: format is confirmed structurally (vertical /shorts/ URL, oEmbed's 113x200 portrait embed, and the prior session's own 720p-scale portrait canvas captures), matching the candidate's own 'single punchy aerial pass' framing, but exact cut count, per-shot length, and transition style (hard cut vs. whip pan vs. speed ramp) could not be reliably determined by either session, so those fields stay null/empty rather than guessed. `review_method` is set to 'live_playback_review' because a genuine live-playback attempt was actually made and did load and inspect real video/canvas frame data on the target page -- not because it yielded cut-count/shot-length data, which per the schema's honesty contract it explicitly did not.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Live playback review via Playwright browser automation (claude-in-chrome extension was not connected, so Playwright MCP tools were used instead) was performed by a prior agent, not this session. Navigated directly to https://www.youtube.com/shorts/ffMC6Fg2pok; confirmed via document.title on each load that the correct video ('Drone Flight From Bon Accord Lagoon to Nylon Pool Tobago...', creator Fishaholic TT) was loaded. One clean in-page JS pass (video paused, then seeked programmatically) read the real HTMLMediaElement.duration = 60.021 seconds -- i.e. this is a full-length (60s) YouTube Short, not a brief ~15s clip. That same pass captured canvas frames at t=3.0s, 15.0s, 30.0s, 45.0s, and 57.0s (roughly 5%/25%/50%/75%/95% through); all five produced substantial non-blank JPEG payloads (30KB-60KB at 160x284px), confirming continuous real aerial footage plays throughout rather than a static title card or black frames at those sample points. That prior session was not able to persist or visually inspect the captured frame images, nor complete a second finer-grained scrub: on every subsequent re-navigation to the identical URL, the page auto-redirected within seconds to unrelated random YouTube Shorts (a Big Bear Mountain first-snow short, a Maldives overwater-suite fly-through short, a city-skyline-at-night short, two different Utah sand-dune shorts) before inspection could finish -- matching a documented anti-bot/feed-redirect pattern seen on other Shorts URLs in this pack. A further retry then hit a 'Browser is already in use' automation-lock error, at which point that session stopped per the review budget (stop rather than rabbit-hole when scrubbing isn't working cleanly). This session (the one authoring this record) did not itself re-run a live-playback pass -- no browser-automation tool was invoked here -- but did independently corroborate, via its own oEmbed fetch, that the source_url's title/creator match exactly, confirming the prior playback session reviewed the correct video. Net result: format is confirmed structurally (vertical /shorts/ URL, ~60s runtime, matches the candidate's own 'single punchy aerial pass' framing), but exact cut count, per-shot length, and transition style (hard cut vs. whip pan vs. speed ramp) could not be reliably determined from either session, so those fields are left null/empty rather than guessed.
